post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 196 (Newbie Game 79)
American Space Tourist Mafia

It is the year 3016. Crazy things have happened. Aliens have been found. However, there is a crisis: a) astronauts are on strike and are refusing to astronaut because all ships sent to said aliens never came back. And b) NASA really needs meatsacks - cough, I mean humans - to go astronaut the aliens cuz The Great American President has threatened to nuke NASA - ahem, I mean, nuke their funding - unless they do, cuz he's a conspiracy nut impatient for Aliens. All seemed lost, until an Unpaid Intern came up with a brilliant idea . . .

"Why don't we send American Tourists?"

Important Rules
  1. This list is just a summary of the most important rules, intended for experienced players or as a reminder.
  2. You may not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  3. Each "day" in this game is 2 weeks in real life while "night" lasts 3 days.
  4. When voting, you must format them correctly. If your vote isn't counted, there is a good chance you messed up the format.
  5. Majority hang is off for Day 1, but it is on starting Day 2.
  6. Do not talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, or this thread, even if you are dead.
  7. This game is dawn start, so Day 1 will not start until everyone confirms, speaking of which, if you've read all the rules below and are ready to start, say trademark in italics in the thread.
  8. All times will be given in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC -5).
  9. If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you will still be warned.
  10. Good luck and have fun.

  • Rules
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas amongst others for the rule template.

    • Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • Do not post at night.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications or anything else.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless you are specifically given permission to do so.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread.
    • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
    • Hide tags are allowed as long as they conform to the above rule.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted, even for things like typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • Do not abuse loopholes.

  • Voting and Hanging
    • This game is based on deadline hang. This means that the player with the most votes at the deadline will be hanged.
    • If two players have the same amount of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that amount of votes first will be hanged.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player reaches a majority of votes, then that player will be hanged.
    • On LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), deadline hang will be turned off. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, which will result in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and "Vote: fDAs" are acceptable votes, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: fdsa", and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly and do not use nicknames. There is one exception to this; see below.
    • For the purpose of voting, l (lowercase L) and I (uppercase i) are identical.
    • Votes in hide or tip tags will not be counted.
    • Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until the mods say the game is officially over. If you want to purposely fake vote, give the mods a heads up in your role PM so it is not accidentally counted.
    • You may vote for a no hang to occur during day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • If there is an even number of players, half of the players voting for no hang is considered a majority, but only for no hang.
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, but if you wish to, unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post, and the game will proceed into twilight.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once the end-of-day scene is posted, the person hanged is officially dead and their role will be revealed. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • All times and deadlines will be given in times currently observed on the U.S. East Coast, namely Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC -5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC -4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day will be 336 hours (14 days). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of the day at their discretion. Note that this will be unlikely and will usually only happen in circumstances of extreme inactivity or replacements too close to end of day.
    • The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
    • The night deadline will be 72 hours (3 days) after night begins.
    • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or their partner(s) (if they have them), the player will lose their chance to perform an action during that night.
    • Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • Night will end some time after the night deadline when the mods make the post announcing the end of night. You may not post until the moderators say you can, even if the night deadline has passed.
    • If a player does not make a game-advancing post in a 48-hour period and they are not in V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence), they will receive an in-game prod.
    • If a player does not make a game-advancing post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and they are not in V/LA, they will receive a PM prod.
    • If a player does not make a game-advancing post in another 36-hour period after receiving a PM prod and they are not in V/LA, they will be replaced, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 5 PM Prods, they will be force replaced.
    • If a player is going to be in V/LA, they must post in bold in-thread or PM the mods. The moderators will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    •  Do not use inappropriate language.
    •  Do not make any personal attacks.
    •  Follow all of the rules.
    •  Be mature.
    •  For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to three warnings, SE’s up to two, and IC’s one. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill-level infraction, they will be modkilled or force-replaced at the mods' discretion. Please do not do anything bad enough to deserve a modkill.

  • Moderators
    • mathjay is the moderator of Mafia Game 196 and HannahW is the other moderator
    • Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderators' posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time.
    • The moderators have the last say in interpreting the rules.

    MG196 OP is based on mathjay formatted ruleset version 1, retroactively designated.
    Current version: 1.final
Setup
This game will use the Backup6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
  1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 Town Universal Backup, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
  2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
  3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
  4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
  5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
  6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Tracker, 1 Town Universal Backup, 2 Mafia Goons
Also there are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified:
  • This game is Dawn start until everyone confirms, at which point it will be Day 1, and the deadline will be 14 days from then.
  • There are no Night 0 actions.
  • Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
  • If applicable, a Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
  • Mafia roleblocker can kill and roleblock in the same night.
  • Cop results are sane.
  • Mafia may only discuss in the mafia Quicktopic during night.

Role PMs
American Space Tourist (Vanilla Townie) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are an American Space Tourist (Vanilla Townie). Congratulations! You have paid unholy sums of money to be upgraded from American Regular Tourist and are ready to reap the rewards of Super Deluxe Uber Prestige Class. Unfortunately, you're still the most vanilla of everyone here. But do you care? Heck no! You're an American Tourist loud and proud and you're gonna take home a fancy overpriced alien souvenir, darnit!

The only mighty and formidable weapons you can use are your voice and your vote.

The thread spaceship can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Chinese Deluxe American Space Tourist (Town Doctor) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are Chinese Deluxe American Space Tourist (Town Doctor). What does your name mean? No one knows, and honestly, you don't really mind cuz no one's gonna pronounce it correctly anyway. However, you are Chinese, and therefore you are a individual of many skills: you can lawyer, you can tennis, and you can also kick butt at the abacus. Crucially, however, your Dear Old Mother also forced you to go to medical school so you can actually doctor pretty good too. Did you have a fun childhood? Nah, but now you get to take names, kick butt, and revive people so that's a Good Deal, la?

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player you want to abrakadabra protect. That person will then be protected from being killed by the Mafia that night.

The thread spaceship can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
A Random Space Wolf (Town Tracker) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are A Random Space Wolf (Town Tracker). Where did you come from? No one knows. Why are you here? No one knows either. How did you get here? No one knows that either and we shall proceed to ignore all important questions in the name of plot, also if you get the reference, yeet. Also, literally no one can tell that you're obviously not human. Or maybe everyone's actually a space wolf and no one can tell. Or maybe Random Space Wolves are Very Normal. Who knows?

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will then stalk track that person for that night, and will be informed who that player visited (targeted), if any.

The thread spaceship can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
American Hugger (Town Jailkeeper) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are American Hugger (Town Jailkeeper). Yay! Everyone knows Americans are the best huggers for reasons that we shall not explicitly state, which is one of the best reasons to be American! And so you are big, you are warm, you are stronk, and you definitely give the Very Best Hugs. In fact, your hugs are so effective, they will completely prevent your victim, ahem, I mean the person you are sharing much love and affection with, from doing anything at all productive that night. But also, they will be completely and utterly safe. Yay hugs!

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. You will squeeze the life out of them roleblock them, preventing them from using their night action, if any, and you will also protect them from being killed by the Mafia. This ability can work on both Town and Mafia.

The thread spaceship can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Suspicious Granny (Town Cop) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are Suspicious Granny (Town Cop). You are very old, and have endured countless hardships. You have learned, the hard way, that the world will not leave you to enjoy your knitting and your fireplace and your American Tourism in peace. So now, you have become Suspicious, and with your spotlessly-clean spectacles, your trusty old cane, and your Very White Hair, you will hunt down God-Awful Annoying Young-Uns over your dead body.

Every night, you may PM me the name of a player. At the end of the night, you will be informed whether they are aligned with the Town or the Mafia, or if your trusty spectacles failed and you were unable to receive a result.

The thread spaceship can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Jack (Universal Backup) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are Jack (Town Universal Backup). Your last name is Ofalltrades. You are very gifted and are naturally good at most things. Unfortunately, you procrastinate way too much to be really ultra mega very ooh-la-la good at anything, isn't that a shame. Tsk tsk tsk.

If a Town-aligned power role dies, you will inherit their powers. However, before that, you are basically a regular American Space Tourist (Vanilla Townie), and your only powers are your voice and your vote.

The thread spaceship can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal should be to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Ambassador of the Society Against Selfie Sticks (Mafia Goon) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are an Ambassador of the Society Against Selfie Sticks (Mafia Goon), also known as SASS. You are also an alien. You heard through the Galactic Grapevine that Earth ran out of proper respectable astronauts to sacrifice and decided to throw despicable American Tourists at the Indomitable Planet of Aga instead. As a very Committed and Hardworking Individual, you have decided to infiltrate their Spaceship and destroy everyone before they infect IPAGA with a deplorable plague of Bad Tourism and Selfie Sticks.

You and your fellow martyr partner, [username], [role name], can choose to kablam kill a Town player every night. During the night, you may discuss in this QuickTopic.

The thread spaceship can be found here.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent it, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town while they try to find you. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!
Actually Smart Ambassador of the Society Against Selfie Sticks (Mafia Roleblocker) wrote:
Hello, [player]!

You are an Actually Smart Ambassador of the Society Against Selfie Sticks (Mafia Roleblocker), also known as SASS. Like your fellow Regular Ambassadors, you really, really hate selfie sticks. Unlike your fellow Regular Ambassadors, however, you actually remembered to bring some Pretty Cool Tools because you are Actually Smart. Honestly though, you just didn't want to die, and you secretly think your fellow SASS Ambassadors are stupid for diving headfirst into a pile of American Tourists. Like honestly, they invented the selfie stick; just think of all the other Horrifically Malignant Objects they could have up their sleeve.

With your partner, [username], [flavor] (Mafia Goon), you can choose to kill a town member every night. In addition, you may target one player every night. If that player performs a night action, it will be blocked.

You and your fellow martyr partner, [username], [role name], can choose to kablam kill a Town player every night. During the night, you may discuss in this QuickTopic. In addition, you may freezeray target one player every night. If that player performs a night action, it will be blocked.

The thread spaceship can be found here.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent it, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town while they try to find you. Play towards your win condition, and good luck!

[/hide]

[hide=Setup]This game will use the [url=https://wiki.mafiascum.net/index.php?title=Backup6]Backup6[/url] setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Before the game starts, the setup will be randomly chosen from 6 possible role combinations:
[list=1][*] 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 Town Universal Backup, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
[*] 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
[*] 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
[*] 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
[*] 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
[*] 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Tracker, 1 Town Universal Backup, 2 Mafia Goons[/list]
Also there are a couple other setup mechanics that should be clarified: 
[list][*] This game is Dawn start until everyone confirms, at which point it will be Day 1, and the deadline will be 14 days from then. 
[*] There are no Night 0 actions. 
[*] Doctors cannot protect themselves two nights in a row.
[*] If applicable, a Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action.
[*] Mafia roleblocker can kill and roleblock in the same night.
[*] Cop results are sane.
[*] Mafia may only discuss in the mafia Quicktopic during night.[/list]

post_rendered:
Everyone thought the American Space Tourist idea was a brilliant idea. Americans were already some of the world's best tourists, risking their lives in pursuit of the perfect selfie, souvenir, "limited edition" abalone ice cream, and whatnot. They were brave, they were courageous, and adding Space into their name - capitalized, Of Course - would only make them cooler.

And so the American Space Tourists set sail on a ship that NASA was too lazy to name, out into the wide open Milky Sea, Sponsored by Verizon and Mars Bars.

Also someone was evidently unhappy with the Unpaid Intern's idea because for some reason, he was dumped on the ship as well, along with his trusty friend, the Other Unpaid Intern. And someone was also evidently supremely unhappy with the whole American Space Tourism shebang cuz the the very next day, both poor unfortunate souls were found on the brink of death.

There was too much sass, gasped mathjay, and died.
He was Unpaid Intern No. 1.
(>ლ), said HannahW, and also died.
She was Unpaid Intern No. 2.

RIP, said the American Space Tourists after a while. There were 9 of them left on the ship. Obviously, evidently, there must've been some Very Questionable Entities amongst those 9. But will the brave American Space Tourists be able to find them in time? Well, tune in in about 3 months to find out! In the meantime, here, have an Ad because this is America!

. . . . . . . . . . . . .


The flavor is long. The gif is annoying. The rules are also long. The gif will be hidden at Day 1 start.
Want Day 1 to come ASAP? Well we have news for you: the confirm rule is xtra ez!
So don't mess it up.
Good luck.

Signed, the Unpaid Interns

Dawn has started at 0:00 EST - Jan 23, 2020.
Day 1 will start when everyone confirms with the confirm rule.

Excitement is good. I'm pretty sure at least.

Some RQS (Random Question Stage):
1. Gender? For pronouns.
2. Preferred nickname? Kind of unintuitive but it can be nice to only type 2-4 characters and have everyone know who you're referring to.

so what we doing

Posting our reactions to what has been said. I also think that in a way this also answers:
NDMath wrote:
@Osmium How do you think we should try to get actual discussion started besides stating weak reads?
Well, starting an actual discussion should be an organic process. It would be pretty boring and even harder to analyze if everyone only stated weak reads. Instead, everyone should eventually end up contributing some body of text that conveys something about their opinions on other people or behaviors, their point of view, or their own tone and tendencies.

Like in the very same post I quoted from ND, he frivolously voted fdas which is clearly a low-effort action. But it is a necessary part of the standard RVS (Random Voting Stage) that people vote others without reason, jokingly at first with dumb reasons, until players start taking offense. In this way, people eventually post something that says something about them, normally to accuse someone of being scummy. Even while being with high probability a bad read, it is enough to be a topic of serious discussion.

So discussions like this one, which do not debate the scumminess or towniness of any players, are on the whole very helpful for my perception of everyone else.

Conclusion: Its probably in town's best interest to encourage discussion so that people aren't inactive and everyone should speak their say on what has happened.

(Okay this is ridiculous, 3 posts in and I'm already nearly writing essays on the philosophy of a game I haven't even played. This is way more effort than I need to expend, so don't expect this in the future. On a more serious note, Vote: yuner)

post_rendered:
BobDBuilder321 wrote:
Also, IC's and EC's (mod and comod too maybe?) I got a question completely generic Mafia question.
When should you role claim/reveal?
There are four main reasons to claim.
1. Mass claim, when everyone claims their role. In 9 player games this typically occurs at lylo but it doesn't have to. Generally later is good.
2. When if you don't claim you're definitely gonna get hung, this applies primarily to power roles as it is bad for town to use a hang on a power role in open/semiopen setups.
3. To CC (counterclaim), this is if someone claims a role which can not exist in the setup because your role exists. The thought is that hanging a mafia is worth outing a pr. (For example if someone claims tracker but you are tracker, or if someone claims doctor but you are universal backup. In both scenarios you know the other person is lying and thus mafia.)
4. Important Results, this is if you're a cop and got a guilty result or a tracker and watched someone visit the person who died.

Wooh, that's it. I think I've got it and this game is already over.
The scumteam is {GrantStar, sudzoof}.

So this began the night before when I decided that I would actually start scumhunting.
I present pieces of evidence in hopefully increasing order of strength. Other reasoning was also used, but to keep things simple, I've left those out.

Early Sudzoof Gut Reads
sudzoof wrote:
well we've got the dream team from 176 so it should be fun
Why does he use the word "fun"? Shouldn't he write something like "town win" or "successful" given that the dream team he's referencing is a star scumhunting team?
sudzoof wrote:
Also I guess I should do the RQS
1. he/him
2. sud
3. No. My most recent town game is 190 and my most recent mafia game is so far off that it doesn't matter for meta purposes at all.
Why does he already start telling people that meta attacks won't work on him? Instead he refuses to post the number which feels like hiding.
sudzoof wrote:
I like the amount of effort put into this post
I also agree with the stuff said in this post
osmium is definitely a person I do not want to be hung d1
I'm probably putting too much worth into one post but its a pretty towny one nonetheless
Even from the beginning of the game I had the distinct impression that this could be pocketing.
sudzoof wrote:
ok you have made reads you will no longer be purged Unvote
I do not want bob to be the hang today
Again, the same "don't want this person to be hanged today" thingy. Why didn't he just townread us? Again smells like pocketing. Pocket the two newbies who are actually capable so that they think you're town.

Ok but that's gut.

Odd Ones Out
Just looking at the group of space tourists, one sees that most people are actually trying to figure things out. Namely Bob, Florina, Olive, ND are all actively trying to read people based on what they've written. Even hotmonkey1 actuaaly posts reads and sounds towny even if everything he write is one sencentence. That only leaves 3 people who aren't: fdas, sudzoof, GrantStar. Fdas is playing true to his meta; doesn't post anything substantial, but whenever he posts something its a valid point. Plus, I really like his #236 and his post #342 strikes me as especially towny considering that scum in that position probably wouldn't be trying to stop a bad bandwagon. So yeah, the only two people not being towny in some way barring fdas.

Ok but being towny doesn't mean much and vice versa.

GrantStar is Scummy
This will overlap partially with reason #4 but bear with me. I simply cannot see how no one else finds it scummy that GrantStar has been lurking this entire game. I've seen him with my own eyes hover on this thread only to leave. People have been telling him to post, contribute, but the only thing people have gotten out of him is some confusion about reads. So lets analyze that infamous post #340 in which GrantStar votes me. Why in the world would the first contributive post that GrantStar makes be a vote with no explanation? At first I considered that it had been because I voted him, but that didnt make sense because Bob had voted him and he didn't react. If GrantStar was town, then if he were to post in response to me it would only make logical sense to defend himself. There do not exist any good reasons for making #340.


The Clincher
Ok, so back to #340 again. What was the motive? Well just look, it was clearly a bandwagon and guess who it was off of? Sudzoof. Of course, the only time GrantStar would post was when was his scumpartner did something and follow his lead. Just copying, as if he was under imaginary orders.


Aftermath: I was now pretty confident in my read, but to clear away some doubts about alternate possibilities, I posted #367 and got a great answer. So then I went ahead and did the scary thing in #369. Lo and behold, sudzoof answers and utterly fails to clear away my doubts about him.

Sudzoof's Response
sudzoof wrote:
...why me specifically?
especially since I'm not going to bother to change your mind since I'm probably voting grant at EoD anyways
Remember that if my theory is correct then in sudzoof's position he would immediately suspect that I was on to both of them. Asking "why me" only confirms the need to ascertain my true intentions and although a town could ask the same thing, I find that it was asked not in a curios manner. He then proceeds to dodge the response with such horrible tone. I asked the question specifically because sudzoof had never indicated his opinion on GrantStar. If sudzoof was town, it makes more sense then that he would be undecided or just read GrantStar as merely being inactive or a noob. But he didn't, instead he makes a pretty smart scum play by throwing GrantStar under if only if to make it seem like he was actually town by ruining my theory that they were scumpartners. So instead of posting towny stuff, sudzoof posts what could be plausibly from a town but at the same time with a clear agenda.
sudzoof wrote:
wait
what is this backtrack
Great, classic deflection. I asked you an honest question and you attacked me back.


But it still wasn't over.

Nail in the Coffin
I knew that now that sudzoof had thrown GrantStar under the bus, GrantStar would post. If sudzoof was town, then why would GrantStar even bother reacting when sudzoof hadn't even explicitly voted him considering his ridiculous inactivity streak. So #373 happens and its a pretty much an admission of guilt. It confirms completely that GrantStar was indeed bandwagoning off of sudzoof. Ask yourselves why GrantStar would even bother to bandwagon off of sudzoof and its clear that there isn't much room for doubting anymore. It didn't matter that sudzoof was an SE, if GrantStar wanted to bandwagon off of SE votes, he would have certainly have done so earlier with ND when ND was making votes with actual reasoning in them that one can follow. Then there's #371. There is both a town way and a scum way of interpreting that. GrantStar got the scum version: "bruh, you just messed up big time."


So now I'm utterly convinced and confident enough to post this massive blob which will seal my fate. I propose voting GrantStar first.

"This is incoherent." Yeah that's my fault. Let me reconstruct the important bit though. Yes, the evening of ND's hang, I was acting scummy. This addresses 3. The rest of the quote answers 2 and 4, since they are the same. You say "Such an extreme desire to have the game go your way is characteristic of mafia", but merely stating so is not enough. Never have you shown that it is not characteristic or even impossible for town. In this case, it is possible. Now, I thought I answered this point already in the original post, but let me reiterate again with extra detail. At the point of ND's hang, it was clear to me that: i) fdas was scum ii) fdas would crusade against me the next day iii) if I failed to show fdas was scummy or lost against him and hung, town would lose and no one would see fdas as scum. These statements are not hard to deduce. For i), simply note that both florina and olive are clearly town. You know who hasn't said olive is clearly town? Fdas. By process of elimination, fdas must be scum. For ii), one could already see how fdas was trying to prop up the possibility that I was bussing GrantStar. He wasn't make any advances towards olive, so that left me, who had already been thrown into a state of semi-opposition against him after I voted fdas. iii) is just true. He'd probably somehow smoothly convince people to hang olive, the same way he just did over ND's hanging. Thus, as far as my logic goes, losing was not an option and fdas had to be hung today, else he would evade the hang tomorrow. So yes, consider the stakes for both town and mafia in this position. They are precisely the same. I realize that this just espouses my lack of confidence in the rest of town, but alas, I feel justified in that opinion.

Now onto 3. I agree. But that is the true narrative. [So deal with it.] Indeed, it jars with the account given above because what type of town tries to ruin their reputation at such a pivotal moment? So yes, it is nutty. I still however would like to flip the question back again. For scum, it is also about as equally nutty. If you don't believe the narrative, then try to construct a better one. [Sorry it had to be this way. At this point, it is hard for me to be helpful to town because of what I did.]

With 5 and 6, I start to have genuine problems with fdas. He took a quote very far out of context in 5. That was quite literally a quote from ND. Regardless, it would be correct to say that I do not see the need for the entire day in order to discuss. I claim that opinion is justified. If anyone noticed what happened last day, most of the time was spent preparing for future LYLO, when that's something you do at LYLO. That is instead of trying to make the correct hang that day (taken from ND without shame). At the beginning of the day, I showed that it was simply necessary to construct a list of 3 people. Of course, the ordering of such a list could change with any new information. But what happened was that fdas was inactive, sudzoof was inactive, and everbody else speculating. I don't see the need for more discussion time when everyone had gone on board with hanging ND and stopped at that. [Don't even mention fdas' claims of wanting time to analyze. If he wants to analyze, then he has all the time he needs to analyze the next day.] [6 is a disgusting allegation.] I throw the question right back. Would fdas have allowed ND not to be hung? It was indeed useless to try to stop the hang. If anything, hanging ND was the right thing to do in order to make sure and cover all suspects.

But now this turns into where I find fault with how fdas has been acting. I know fdas knows what a good argument looks like. See:
fdas wrote:
I am not really convinced by Osmium's argument. I can see that Grant is being uncontributive and lazy, and that can be scummy. But the later parts don't connect.

I see nothing wrong with sudzoof's response. Osmium specifically @sudzoof and no one else and asked for his opinion on Grant. It is totally reasonable for sudzoof to ask why Osmium was fixated on him specifically. If I was the one pinged I too would have questioned it as there is no good reason for one person to be singled out. And if sudzoof really did intend on voting Grantstar, he would be very confused by someone asking him to argue for the opposite. A lot of this reasoning seems tainted by confirmation bias.

I took this post to heart because it was true; there was a lot of confirmation bias. So then, I cannot reconcile the current way fdas is arguing with how an imagined town fdas would act, considering that they know how to construct a proper argument. Let us revisit 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 again and look at the language used: "extreme", "nuttiest", "obsession". These aren't words you can use lightly. They evidence a deliberate attempt to portray me negatively that ignores facts. Reread 5, it reminds me of my own post where I would assign motives to sudzoof that didn't really follow from what he said. Was I bent on making sure that town doesn't get discussion time? Clearly not as extremely as fdas makes it look, and here fdas is again refusing to stick to evidence. What is with the accusatory tone in 6? Since when did cutting discussion time become a crime of most grievous offense? I stand by the fact that hanging ND was the right thing to do, but what most worries me is the fact that I had already answered the allegation. Throughout that post and others, fdas completely ignores the actual substance of what I had said in defence. It fits the definition of cherry-picking. Fdas attacks how I played, but never actually discusses the logic I provided behind them. The only thing he says of my reasoning is that having a strong desire to lead town is just wrong, even when I had already explained that too. I'm not done, take the previous attacking post:
fdas wrote:
Osmium was acting very scummy at the end of day yesterday. He expressed a strong desire to railroad town into following exactly the plan that was laid out, which shows that he might be a mafia who wants to make sure town sticks completely to the course of action that would see him win the game.
Osmium_ wrote:
And introduce unpredictability?

Mafia hates unpredictability. They want to push the game into a state where they are winning and let it run its course. Town wants to actually find scum and deciding to refuse to consider any more information is not conducive to that. The fact that Osmium is so afraid of unpredictability shows that is afraid that town might actually change their mind and the free win he once saw by bussing his teammate evaporates.

After Osmium realized he acted scummy, he immediately tried to excuse his actions by claiming it was a reaction test.
Osmium_ wrote:
It means that I've purposely made myself appear scummy and only fdas had the correct reaction to that.

This is such a lazy and unverifiable response to anything anyone could have done that is scummy. After doing something scummy, he backtracks and says that it was a reaction test. This is not sincere. Osmium has something to hide.

Is this all fdas can do? Equate my actions with mafia motivations? I agree that it was scummy. But why does fdas have to take a sincere and logical quote, "And introduce unpredictability", and immediately deny that it makes any logical sense for town even when it truly does as already explained by me in the response. Unpredictability is bad if the hang is the only logical choice based upon clear evidence. To make matters worse, fdas then just writes assuming I was scum as if it was obvious. He proceeds to write that I was "afraid that the bussing plot might be discovered" when there is absolutely no evidence for that. Again it reminds me of what I did to sudzoof, voicing out loud what I thought were sudzoof's mafia motivations were as fact, when they really were not. The entire post is laced with this utter confidence that I was mafia, which is not wrong, but is instead used to create narratives of me that ignore facts. Rereading again my attack against sudzoof, it is very far-fetched to say that it was all an attempt to buss GrantStar.

To wrap up my [indictment] of fdas, I simply revisit what happened after I voted fdas. Fdas immediately took away any position I had the moment I did so. From never really taking an active role, fdas rose to enforce hanging ND with great effect. So when fdas accuses me of not standing up to my beliefs about ND, I say it was his fault. Just take a look at his reasoning for doing so. Fdas completely ignores and glosses over what I've said behind why I stopped voting ND, by pushing a concept that scum will always appear towny to the very end, which doesn't entirely dismiss my case. It is however, a good reason to be cautious and vote ND regardless, which is not how he phrases it. Instead fdas, makes these two claims for why he is so confident that ND is GrantStar's scumbuddy; i) "NDMath was hard defending Grant for way too long" ii) "Later on, he tried his best to push the hang onto sudzoof instead of Grant." Simply by rereading, neither of these two claims hold any water. NDMath has not been defending GrantStar for his actions, and there are exactly two posts in which ND can be construed as making an effort to not let GranstStar be hanged. Fdas mentions both of them, including the supposed gem that is #343. But neither is strong evidence in themselves, merely as fdas assigns a shared motive for both of them and ignores whatever ND says about why he preferred to hang sudzoof or attack my motive in proposing a GrantStar hang. There isn't the continued support of GrantStar that fdas makes it out to be. But, fdas made a public display of confronting ND, and made it look ridiculous to think otherwise.

And that is why fdas' continued attitude over the past week seriously worries me and should worry everyone else. While what fdas says sounds good, take the time to seriously analyze his arguments. [This is completely irrelevant, but I love how fdas switches around from "We should treat the idea that Osmium was bussing seriously" to "Osmium's open-mindedness is towny" to "Osmium's determination to stick to the plan is indication of mafia"].
So what now? After 3:00 PM Monday, if everyone responds and no one unvotes, then I will hang myself and prove my innocence. I see no point in wasting time if everything looks set for my hanging. [Also to spite fdas and his need of time to analyze.]

Reread 5, it reminds me of my own post where I would assign motives to sudzoof that didn't really follow from what he said. Was I bent on making sure that town doesn't get discussion time? Clearly not as extremely as fdas makes it look, and here fdas is again refusing to stick to evidence. What is with the accusatory tone in 6? Since when did cutting discussion time become a crime of most grievous offense? I stand by the fact that hanging ND was the right thing to do, but what most worries me is the fact that I had already answered the allegation. Throughout that post and others, fdas completely ignores the actual substance of what I had said in defence. It fits the definition of cherry-picking. Fdas attacks how I played, but never actually discusses the logic I provided behind them. The only thing he says of my reasoning is that having a strong desire to lead town is just wrong, even when I had already explained that too. I'm not done, take the previous attacking post: 

[quote=fdas]
Osmium was acting very scummy at the end of day yesterday. He expressed a strong desire to railroad town into following exactly the plan that was laid out, which shows that he might be a mafia who wants to make sure town sticks completely to the course of action that would see him win the game.

[quote=Osmium_]And introduce unpredictability?[/quote]

Mafia hates unpredictability. They want to push the game into a state where they are winning and let it run its course. Town wants to actually find scum and deciding to refuse to consider any more information is not conducive to that. The fact that Osmium is so afraid of unpredictability shows that is afraid that town might actually change their mind and the free win he once saw by bussing his teammate evaporates.

After Osmium realized he acted scummy, he immediately tried to excuse his actions by claiming it was a reaction test.

[quote=Osmium_]It means that I've purposely made myself appear scummy and only fdas had the correct reaction to that.[/quote]

This is such a lazy and unverifiable response to anything anyone could have done that is scummy. After doing something scummy, he backtracks and says that it was a reaction test. This is not sincere. Osmium has something to hide.[/quote]

Is this all fdas can do? Equate my actions with mafia motivations? I agree that it was scummy. But why does fdas have to take a sincere and logical quote, "And introduce unpredictability", and immediately deny that it makes any logical sense for town even when it truly does as already explained by me in the response. Unpredictability is bad if the hang is the only logical choice based upon clear evidence. To make matters worse, fdas then just writes assuming I was scum as if it was obvious. He proceeds to write that I was "afraid that the bussing plot might be discovered" when there is absolutely no evidence for that. Again it reminds me of what I did to sudzoof, voicing out loud what I thought were sudzoof's mafia motivations were as fact, when they really were not. The entire post is laced with this utter confidence that I was mafia, which is not wrong, but is instead used to create narratives of me that ignore facts. Rereading again my attack against sudzoof, it is very far-fetched to say that it was all an attempt to buss GrantStar.

To wrap up my [indictment] of fdas, I simply revisit what happened after I voted fdas.  Fdas immediately took away any position I had the moment I did so. From never really taking an active role, fdas rose to enforce hanging ND with great effect. So when fdas accuses me of not standing up to my beliefs about ND, I say it was his fault. Just take a look at his reasoning for doing so. Fdas completely ignores and glosses over what I've said behind why I stopped voting ND, by pushing a concept that scum will always appear towny to the very end, which doesn't entirely dismiss my case. It is however, a good reason to be cautious and vote ND regardless, which is not how he phrases it. Instead fdas, makes these two claims for why he is so confident that ND is GrantStar's scumbuddy; i) "NDMath was hard defending Grant for way too long" ii) "Later on, he tried his best to push the hang onto sudzoof instead of Grant." Simply by rereading, neither of these two claims hold any water. NDMath has not been defending GrantStar for his actions, and there are exactly two posts in which ND can be construed as making an effort to not let GranstStar be hanged. Fdas mentions both of them, including the supposed gem that is #343. But neither is strong evidence in themselves, merely as fdas assigns a shared motive for both of them and ignores whatever ND says about why he preferred to hang sudzoof or attack my motive in proposing a GrantStar hang. There isn't the continued support of GrantStar that fdas makes it out to be. But, fdas made a public display of confronting ND, and made it look ridiculous to think otherwise. 

And that is why fdas' continued attitude over the past week seriously worries me and should worry everyone else. While what fdas says sounds good, take the time to seriously analyze his arguments. [This is completely irrelevant, but I love how fdas switches around from "We should treat the idea that Osmium was bussing seriously" to "Osmium's open-mindedness is towny" to "Osmium's determination to stick to the plan is indication of mafia"].

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
So what now? After 3:00 PM Monday, if everyone responds and no one unvotes, then I will hang myself and prove my innocence. I see no point in wasting time if everything looks set for my hanging. [Also to spite fdas and his need of time to analyze.]

So it has come to this. I do not understand your reluctance to vote completely, as both fdas and I would agree that we've already made the points that we wanted to make. I was ready to exit the game last weekend, but florina said no, so now I'm still here, and unsure of why that is. I understand that you were practicing for AIME (did it go well?), but we could have saved a lot of time here. To rehash some points, hopefully crystal clear now:

Defense: I'm actually curious as to why fdas picked the quote he did to present his argument last post, as I feel those were the points I had a better response too. Feeling a strong desire to have the game progress your way, I would agree is a warning sign, but certainly town isn't excluded from feeling this way either. Any town should have a strong desire to hang who they think is clearly scum. Then there's also the fact that I was not trying to stick to the plan when I voted fdas, I had advocated to hang NDMath from the very start, so my behavior clearly isn't of an individual that doesn't want to consider any more information that is not conducive to the plan. Even fdas was praising me at times for having an open mind. The mess that happened after I hung NDMath, is of course difficult to explain, and if you want to use that as reason to vote me, then do so. However anything I said during that string of posts makes no sense, was incoherent, did not abide by my usual standards of posting, and looked stupid. To the point where I find that the possibility of scum or town making those posts is equally ridiculous. Now to the more contentious point brought up earlier of why I voted NDMath in the end. I considered ND more likely town than not. Yet if it was the case that my readings of his posting style were wrong and we mishung fdas, then it was going to be nearly impossible to get NDMath hanged the next day because I had just claimed he had to be town. (I believe I said something along these lines earlier that day.) On the other hand, and now this overlaps with Offense, fdas had made it quite nearly impossible to not have NDMath hanged. So to me there was no point in trying to change that fact.

Offense: It was only when I stopped voting NDMath, that fdas (and actually sudzoof as well) made a case for voting NDMath. He could've done that since the beginning of the day, but instead he starts putting huge effort into making sure that NDMath looked unequivocally scum. And as I've made the point before in the earlier post, his arguments were actually pretty weak. Fdas should not be putting that much effort into hanging NDMath when he was just exaggerating how much NDMath was defending GrantStar. Fdas keeps calling me out for hanging NDMath, even when he was the reason we were already mostly there. Fdas acts as if he has nothing to defend against, that what he did in pushing NDMath's hang was justified. He has never responded to any of my points against him fully. Instead he has simply written that it was his job to portray me negatively because he thinks I'm scum, which is to say that he somehow has the liscense to argue poorly. I've already shown in that previous post, how much fdas' arguments don't stick to the evidence and extrapolate too much.

So then, you may not agree with my decisions, you may also have misgivings about the way I posted at the end of D2, but fdas remains scummy. I don't understand why no else seems to find fault with him.

I'm supposed to be helping my wincon so: Vote: fdas

post_rendered:
Once upon a time, something happened, and I'm not sure what this is. Thanks to Unpaid Intern No. 2 for most of the flavor today.

EXCLUSIVE, SHOCKING, SCANDALOUS LOOK INTO THE LIFE OF AN INTERN! YOU WILL NOT BELIEVE YOU'RE EYES! WHAT?!
Unpaid Intern No 2 drops her notebook on the table. "Ok, so... what do you think?"

"I think that I hate the world. It's horrible. Utterly awful. Bad. Gross. Disgusting." Unpaid Intern No. 1 proceeds to melt into the floor crying. "My student loans... My jobs... Noooo... TT^TT"

"..." said Unpaid Intern No. 2. "Look, if we get this article done right-" She coughs. "-they promised that we'd become paid interns number 1 and 2."

Unpaid Intern No. 1 rolls his eyes. "Wow, what a huge improvement, my life is changed forever I can just tell. JUST LET ME SLEEP YOU FOOLS," he yells at the heckflame sky (ceiling?) of Heck, shaking his fist at... something.

"Who are you shaking your fist at?" Unpaid Intern No. 2 sighs and tries vainly to conjure some lead out of her mechanical pencil.

"Life. The universe. And the Heccing Americans."

"You're dead already. "

"Oh boo hoo, youre not dead if youre still suffering are you."

"Plus, the last time you went on a rampage complaining about the Americans... that article was a mess," she said. "All we have to do now is do something decent. "

"What?" Unpaid Intern No. 1 sat up and looked supremely affronted. "Excuse you that article was brilliant for something written at 4 am in the morning thank you very much," he sniffs.

* smh *, says Unpaid Intern No. 2. "Hm... At any rate, try to be responsible... and maybe the life--or death--of an intern won't be so bad."

"No. God, I hate those R things. Responsibility," Unpaid Intern No. 1 says, ticking the atrocities off on his fingers. "Remonstrance. Remorse. Repercussions. Russians. Racism. And irony."

"Irony?"

"Irony yes. Also you're deluding yourself because the life and death of an intern shall and always be absolutely horrible.""

"By the way, I believe you forgot reality and retail. You mentioned those as well last time."

"Oh gosh dang darn it why'd you have to remind me? Urgh..."

Unpaid Intern No. 2 tut-tuts. "At any rate, it is kind of ironical that we're stuck down here in the underworld being unsuccessful interns because of our great success as interns up there." She smhs. "If you want to complain, it was your idea to send American Tourists into space. That's what got us killed and what made people down here make us interns in the first place -- because they thought we were good."

"Yea," Unpaid Intern No. 1 replies. "Therefore never have good ideas. Boom badabing badaboom problem solved."

"Too late. Why don't you produce some good ideas now. What should we write about all that drama stuff in space."

"Just say blah blah blah AmErIcAn bleh bleh bleh tRaDeMaRk yadda yadda yadda dIeD oh hey there sudzoof."

"Ah," says Unpaid Intern No. 2. "If no one else knows that sudzoof is dead yet, we can get our article there. Night's not over yet, is it?"

"Oh but it will be. Soon. Ehehehee," he says, chuckling. "And then I can rest. Forever." He pauses, thinking. "At least, until something else happens. It usually does. Like people bullying me. T^T I'm sad why do I keep getting bullied..."

Unpaid Intern No. 2 glares at Unpaid Intern No. 1. "Pay attention," she says.

"Your sympathy is appreciated."

Unpaid Intern No. 2 ignores him and calls over sudzoof. "What happened?" she asks, her pencil poised over her notebook.

Unpaid Intern No. 1 snorts. "He died. Obviously." He swipes Unpaid Intern No. 2's notebook, before raising his eyebrows. "Daheck is this. Roleplay?"

Unpaid Intern No. 2 blinks, before snatching her notebook back. "No. Just my speculations of what's happening up there in space. Why does everything look like a game to you." She smhs disapprovingly before turning back to sudzoof.

"So...?"

A Decent Article wrote:
Late last night--or should I say early this morning--sudzoof died.

At exactly midnight, sudzoof attempted to take a selfie of him apparently falling out of an airlock. However, he in fact actually fell out of the airlock. He died.

On the other hand, sudzoof brightly notes that he did manage to take a selfie of himself falling out of an airlock before his death. He explains that the reason that he had attempted such a feat in the first place was because he wanted to establish that The Best Selfie needed to involve a life or death situation. He put that together with American Space Tourism and--lasjdflajsdlfkasjdf--falling out of an airlock.

Very Sophisticated Scientists have carefully studied the scene of death and have concluded that someone intentially pushed sudzoof out. The details are yet to be disclosed, but rumors are that it was a jealous contender for the award for The Best Selfie 20XX. A small minority maintains that it was an alien infiltrator.

Sudzoof was unavailable to comment on this. He was last seen in the underworld this morning, walking away from Formerly Unpaid Interns No 1 and 2 and very pleased at his significant contribution to Selfie Theory.

sudzoof has died. He was an American Space Tourist (Vanilla Townie).

It is now Day 4. You may post in this thread. Mafia may no longer discuss in their Quicktopic.

Day 4 will end on March 30, 2020 - 18:00 EDT
